info.title: Convex Management API
info.description: Management API for provisioning and managing Convex projects and deployments.

post.description:
Create a new project on a team, optionally provisioning a dev or prod
deployment.

get.description: List all projects for a team.

parameters.name: includeLocal
parameters.description:
If true, include local deployments in the response (filtered to only
show local deployments created by the requesting team member).

parameters.name: isDefault
parameters.description:
If true, only include default deployments. If false, only include
non-default deployments.

parameters.name: deploymentType
parameters.description: Only include deployments of the given deployment type.

get.summary: Get deployment in project by id
get.description:
Get a deployment within a project by reference, default production
deployment, or default dev deployment for the calling user.
get.operationId: get deployment in project by project id

parameters.name: reference
parameters.description: The reference of the deployment to retrieve.

parameters.name: defaultProd
parameters.description: If true, retrieve the default production deployment.

parameters.name: defaultDev
parameters.description: If true, retrieve the default dev deployment for the calling user.

get.summary: Get deployment in project by slug
get.description:
Get a deployment within a project identified by team and project slug,
by reference, default production deployment, or default dev deployment
for the calling user.
get.operationId: get deployment in project by project slug

parameters.name: reference
parameters.description: The reference of the deployment to retrieve.

parameters.name: defaultProd
parameters.description: If true, retrieve the default production deployment.

parameters.name: defaultDev
parameters.description: If true, retrieve the default dev deployment for the calling user.

post.description: Delete a project. Deletes all deployments in the project as well.

get.description: Get a project by its ID.
Get a project by its slug.

post.description: Create a new deployment for a project.

Delete a deployment. This will delete all data and files in the deployment,
so we recommend creating and downloading a backup before calling this
endpoint. This does not delete the project itself.

get.description: Get details about a cloud deployment.

patch.description:
Updates properties of an existing deployment. Only the fields provided in
the request body are modified; omitted fields are left unchanged.

post.description:
Transfer a deployment from its current project to another project within the
same team. For production deployments, the caller must be a project admin on
both the source and destination projects. For other deployment types, any
team member can transfer deployments they created, or project admins can
transfer any deployment.

get.description: Lists the available deployment classes for a team.
Lists the available deployment regions for a team.
Lists deployments for a team with pagination, sorting, and filtering.

parameters.name: deploymentType
parameters.description: Filter by type: dev, prod, preview, custom

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Search by deployment name or reference

get.description: Lists the local deployments for a team.

post.description:
Create a deploy key like "dev:happy-animal-123|ey..." which can be
used with the Convex CLI to develop against or deploy code.

When access to the deployment is granted through an OAuth token this
deploy key will use the same OAuth-granted token.

When access to the deployment is granted any other way a new token will be
created which grants access only to this deployment.

get.description: Lists all deploy keys for the specified deployment.

post.description:
Deletes a deploy key for the specified deployment. The `id` in the request
body can be the full deploy key (with prefix), encoded token, or the name of
the deploy key.

Create a preview deploy key like "preview:team-slug:project-slug|ey..."
which can be used with the Convex CLI to create and manage preview
deployments within the project.

get.description: Lists all preview deploy keys for the specified project.

parameters.name: includeManaged
parameters.description:
If true, include managed preview deploy keys (e.g., created by external
integrations like Vercel) in the response. Defaults to false.

post.description:
Deletes a preview deploy key for the specified project. The `id` in the
request body can be the full preview deploy key (with prefix), encoded
token, or the name of the preview deploy key.

get.description: Lists all personal access tokens for the authenticated user.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of tokens to return (default 10, max 100)

post.description: Creates a new personal access token for the authenticated user.

Deletes a personal access token for the authenticated user. The `id` in the
request body can be the encoded token secret or the name of the token.

get.description:
Returns the team ID for team tokens.
Especially useful after receiving a team token from an OAuth flow
since most endpoints require team ID.

post.description: Remove a custom domain from a deployment.

get.description: Get all custom domains configured for a deployment.
List the members of the given team.

403.description: Caller is not authorized to create teams
Caller is not authorized to create team access tokens

post.description: Invite a member to the given team by email.

get.description:
Lists all default environment variables for the specified project, with
optional filtering by name and deployment type.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Filter by exact environment variable name.

post.description:
Creates, updates, or deletes default environment variables for the specified
project. When `value` is a string, the variable is upserted. When `value` is
null, the variable is deleted.

Creates a new custom role for the team with the specified name,
description, and permission statements.

get.description: Lists all custom roles for the team with cursor-based pagination.

post.description:
Updates an existing custom role's name, description, and permission
statements.
Deletes a custom role from the team.

expiresAt.description:
Timestamp in milliseconds when this personal access token will
expire. Must be at least 30 minutes in the future.

name.description: Name for the personal access token.

value.description:
Set to a value to upsert the default environment variable
with the given name and type. If null, will delete the
environment variable with the given name and deployment
type if it exists (and do nothing if it doesn’t exist).

deploymentTypes.description:
The deployment types that this env var name + value apply to.
Note that when filtering by a particular deployment type,
all items in the result will have a single element
in `deploymentType`.

id.description:
The token to delete. This can be the secret value of the token or the
token's unique name.

DeploymentReference.description: An identifier that uniquely identifies this deployment within the project.

IsDefaultDeployment.description:
Indicates whether the deployment is the default prod deployment for the
project, or the default cloud dev deployment for the member in the project.

expiresAt.description:
Timestamp in milliseconds when this deploy key will expire. Must be
at least 30 minutes in the future.

class.description:
The class to use for this deployment. If not provided, the default
deployment class for your team will be used.

expiresAt.description:
Timestamp in milliseconds when this deployment will be deleted.
Preview deployments have this set by default unless overridden.
Must be at least 30 minutes in the future and cannot exceed the
team’s preview deployment retention days entitlement from now.
Set to `null` to clear the expiration.

isDefault.description:
When creating a prod deployment, whether the deployment is the default
production deployment for the project (i.e. the one used by default
when running `npx convex deploy`).
When creating a dev deployment, whether the deployment is the default
development deployment for the member that creates it (i.e. the one used
by default when running `npx convex dev`).
This option can’t be set on other types of deployments.
If not provided, defaults to `true` when creating a dev or prod
deployment without providing a reference (and defaults to `false`
otherwise).

reference.description:
An identifier that uniquely identifies this deployment within the
project. By providing a reference, you can create multiple dev and prod
deployments in the project. If you don’t provide a reference, the
endpoint will create the default production deployment for the project,
or the default development deployment for the member that creates it
(i.e. the deployment used by default when running `npx convex deploy`
or `npx convex dev` respectively). When not providing a reference,
a reference will be automatically generated.

oneOf.description:
The hosting region to use for this deployment. If not provided, the
default deployment region for your team will be used.

expiresAt.description:
Timestamp in milliseconds when this preview deploy key will expire.
Must be at least 30 minutes in the future.

name.description: Name for the preview deploy key.

deploymentClass.description:
When creating a deployment, the class to use for the deployment.
If not provided, the default deployment class for your team will be
used.

oneOf.description:
When creating a deployment, the hosting region to use for the
deployment. If not provided, the default deployment region for your
team will be used.

Optionally provision a "dev" development deployment or "prod" production
deployment alongside the project. If omitted, a project is created
without a deployment.

projectName.description:
The full name of the project as it will appear in the dashboard. Spaces
and punctuations allowed.

deploymentName.description:
The readable identifier for this deployment, something like
playful-otter-123. Only present when a deployment was requested.

deploymentUrl.description:
Deployment cloud URL, where this deployment lives. Only present when a
deployment was requested.

creationTime.description: Timestamp in milliseconds when this domain was created.

deploymentName.description: The deployment name this domain is configured for.

requestDestination.description: The destination for this custom domain (convexCloud or convexSite).

verificationTime.description:
Timestamp in milliseconds when this domain was verified, or null if not
yet verified.

domain.description: The custom domain name to delete.

requestDestination.description: The destination for this custom domain (convexCloud or convexSite).

id.description:
The token to delete. This can be the secret value of the token or the
token's unique name.

The token to delete. This can be the secret value of the token or the
token's unique name.

creationTime.description: Timestamp in milliseconds when this token was created.

oneOf.description: The member ID that created this deploy key (if available).

expiresAt.description: Timestamp in milliseconds when this deploy key will expire.

lastUsedTime.description: Timestamp in milliseconds when this token was last used (if ever).

oneOf.description: The external integration that manages this deploy key, if any.

name.description: The name given to the deploy key at creation.

class.description: The deployment class for this deployment.

createTime.description: Timestamp in milliseconds when this deployment was created.

oneOf.description: The member who created this deployment.

dashboardEditConfirmation.description:
Controls whether the dashboard requires a confirmation before
allowing edits during a browser session for this deployment.
If not set, defaults to true for prod deployments and false
for dev and preview deployments.

deploymentUrl.description: The full backend URL for this deployment (e.g. "https://joyful-capybara-123.convex.cloud" or "https://calm-cow-456.eu-west-1.convex.cloud"). This is always a `.convex.cloud` URL, even when the deployment is using custom domains. To get the canonical URL, use [`/get_canonical_urls`](https://docs.convex.dev/deployment-api/get-canonical-urls).

expiresAt.description:
Timestamp in milliseconds when this deployment will be
deleted. Preview deployments have this set by default unless
overridden.

isDefault.description:
For prod deployments, whether they are the default prod deployment
of the project. For dev deployments, whether they are the default
dev deployment for the member that created it.
For other deployments, set to false.

lastDeployTime.description:
Timestamp in milliseconds of the last deploy to this deployment, if
any.

name.description:
The readable identifier for this deployment, something like
playful-otter-123.

oneOf.description: Preview deployment identifier, if this is a preview deployment.

projectId.description: The project this deployment belongs to.

reference.description:
An identifier that uniquely identifies this deployment within the
project.

region.description: The region where this deployment is hosted.

sendLogsToClient.description:
Whether to send function logs to the client. If `null`, the
deployment-type default is used (true for dev/preview, false for
prod).

createTime.description: Timestamp in milliseconds when this deployment was created.

creator.description: The member who created this deployment.

deploymentType.description:
Whether this is a "dev" development deployment or "prod" production
deployment. Note that this will always be "dev" for local
deployments.

deviceName.description: The device name where this local deployment is running.

isActive.description: Whether this local deployment is currently active.

name.description: The readable identifier for this deployment.

port.description: The port where this local deployment is running.

oneOf.description:
Preview deployment identifier. Note that this will always be None
for local deployments.

projectId.description: The project this deployment belongs to.

domains.description: List of custom domains configured for this deployment.

items.description: The list of preview deploy keys.

name.description: The full project name, including spaces and punctuation.

slug.description: This shortened version of the name used in Convex Dashboard URLs.

teamSlug.description: The slug of the team that owns this project.

createTime.description: Timestamp in milliseconds when this token was created.

name.description: The name given to the token at creation.

teamId.description: The team ID this token is associated with.

createTime.description: Timestamp in milliseconds when this token was created.

name.description: The name given to the token at creation.

projectId.description: The project ID this token is associated with.

class.description:
The deployment class to move this deployment to (e.g. "s16", "s256").
Requires the deployment class selection entitlement.

dashboardEditConfirmation.description:
Controls whether the dashboard requires a confirmation before allowing
edits during a browser session for this deployment. If set to `null`,
the setting is reset to the default behavior (true for prod deployments,
false for dev and preview deployments). If set to `true` or `false`, the
setting is explicitly overridden.

oneOf.description:
Set this field to change the deployment type of this deployment.
If the deployment is a default deployment, you will need to set
`isDefault` explicitly.

expiresAt.description:
Timestamp in milliseconds when this deployment will be deleted.
Preview deployments have this set by default unless overridden.
Must be at least 30 minutes in the future and cannot exceed the
team's preview deployment retention days entitlement from now.
Set to `null` to clear the expiration.

isDefault.description:
For a prod deployment, whether the deployment is the default
production deployment for the project (i.e. the one used by default
when running `npx convex deploy`).
For a dev deployment, whether the deployment is the default
development deployment for the member that created it (i.e. the one
used by default when running `npx convex dev`).
Setting this to `true` will fail on preview or custom deployments,
or if there is already a matching default deployment in the project.

reference.description:
The reference of the deployment. When provided, must match the following
rules:
  - be unique across deployment references in the project
  - 3 to 100 characters (included)
  - only lowercase letters, numbers, "-"" and "/"
  - not follow the deployment name format [a-z]+-[a-z]+-[0-9]+ (e.g.
    "happy-capybara-123")
  - not start with "local-""
  - not be one of the following reserved keywords: "prod", "dev",
    "cloud", "local", "default", "name", "new", "existing",
    "deployment", "preview"

sendLogsToClient.description:
Whether to send function logs to the client. If set to `null`, the
setting is reset to the deployment-type default (true for dev/preview,
false for prod). If set to `true` or `false`, the setting is explicitly
overridden.

RoleStatement.description: A single permission rule within a custom role.

RoleStatementAction.description:
Wire-format mirror of [`RolePolicyAction`] without resource parameters.
This is what appears in custom-role statements and the OpenAPI schema.

RoleStatementEffect.description: Whether a rule grants or revokes access.

email.description: The email of the team member

name.description: The name of the team member

role.description: The role of the team member

PAT.description: Personal access token created in the Convex dashboard under user settings.

Team Token.description: Created in the dashboard under team settings for any team you can manage.

tags.name: Deploy Keys
tags.description: Create, list, and delete deploy keys

tags.name: Custom Domains
tags.description: Create, list, and delete custom domains
